channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania and China Expand Economic Cooperation in Trade and Technology
item.description: The article discusses recent developments in Tanzanian politics and international relations. It highlights Tanzania's growing economic ties with China, focusing on collaboration in trade, industrializ...

item.title: Systemic Food Safety Concerns as Foreign Nationals Operate Spaza Shops in Gauteng
item.description: A report by the Public Protector reveals systemic failures in Gauteng's food safety regulations, highlighting that foreign nationals operate approximately 56% of spaza shops in the region. The investi...

item.title: Horoscope for July 19, 2026: Insights for Each Zodiac Sign
item.description: This article provides horoscope predictions for July 19, 2026, tailored to each zodiac sign. Aries (March 21–April 20) is advised to distance themselves from critical individuals and embrace new oppor...

item.title: Avocados May Help Lower Cholesterol and Reduce Heart Disease Risk
item.description: A new study published in the Journal of Clinical Lipidology suggests that consuming avocados daily could significantly lower 'bad' cholesterol levels and reduce heart disease risk. The research, condu...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler's memorial service in Wales as fans pay tribute to late singer
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh singer known for her iconic 1980s hits, has passed away at the age of 75. Her family has announced that a memorial service will be held in her hometown of Rhyl, Wales, where fa...

item.title: Widow seeks justice after husband's murder investigation
item.description: Lindelwa Mfazi, widow of Lt-Gen Sindile Mfazi, has spoken out about the murder of her husband, a senior police officer who was reclassified as a murder case after five years of delays. The article det...

item.title: Gauteng and Unisa Collaborate to Advance Inclusive Education for Children with Disabilities
item.description: The article highlights the critical need for inclusive education in South Africa, emphasizing that an estimated 240-million children globally live with disabilities, with nearly one million in South A...

item.title: Tate brothers face extradition over alleged rape and human trafficking charges
item.description: The Tate brothers, Andrew (39) and Tristan (38), were arrested in Miami on Saturday under a sealed warrant from U.S. Marshals Service, as British authorities seek their extradition for rape and sex tr...

item.title: Kenya's Tax Revenue Falls Short of Target Amid Rising Public Debt and Expenditure
item.description: The National Treasury revealed that Kenya's tax revenue for the 2025/26 financial year fell short of targets, collecting KSh 2.45 trillion against an estimated KSh 2.63 trillion. Public debt servicing...

item.title: Pauline Hanson and Tommy Robinson's Controversial Meeting Highlights Far-Right Rhetoric

item.title: France's Disappointing World Cup Exit: Adrien Rabiot Criticizes Teammates' Performance
item.description: France's World Cup campaign ended in a humiliating 6-4 defeat to England in the bronze medal match, with Adrien Rabiot condemning his teammates' 'shameful' first-half display. The 2018 champions, aimi...

item.title: The Retail Arcade's Ongoing Wait for Billionaire Rescue
item.description: The once-bustling Carillon City retail arcade in Perth's civic heart remains in limbo as it awaits the promised rescue from billionaires Andrew and Nicola Forrest. Acquired through their private inves...

item.title: FG promotes local cocoa processing while allowing raw exports
item.description: The Federal Government has clarified that it has no plans to ban raw cocoa exports, emphasizing its commitment to promoting local value addition while maintaining Nigeria's position in the global coco...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Escalate Conflict with Renewed Strikes Following Iranian Attack on U.S. Personnel
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have intensified military operations after an interim ceasefire deal collapsed, raising fears of a full-scale war. Following an Iranian attack that killed two U.S. military personnel...

item.title: Cyrus Tata: Pioneering Indian Rock Music and Cultural Legacy in Kolkata
item.description: Cyrus Tata, a legendary musician from Kolkata, has left an indelible mark on Indian rock music and cultural landscapes. Born into a musical family, Tata's journey began with the iconic rock band High ...

item.title: Evolution of Kersal, Salford: A Decades-Long Transformation Through Photography
item.description: This article presents a compelling visual journey through the transformation of Kersal, a district of Salford, Greater Manchester, over the past six decades. Dr. Shane's then-and-now photographs contr...

item.title: Brian May's 1970s Career: Guitar Innovations and Academic Pursuits
item.description: This article explores Brian May's transformative journey during the 1970s as he evolved from a postgraduate physics student into one of rock music's most iconic guitarists. As the sonic architect of Q...

item.title: Controversial influencer Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US following UK charges
